paper.title: Fundamental Parameters for Central Stars of 103 Infrared Bowshock Nebulae

extraction.summary: The paper identifies 19 bowshock stars as runaways (v_2D >= 25 km/s) but does not discuss any objects as possibly unbound from the Milky Way or as hypervelocity star candidates. Maximum 2D peculiar velocities are ~40 km/s, far below Galactic escape velocities. No HVS/unbound candidates are included.

candidate_groups_considered.description: 19 bowshock stars classified as runaways based on 2D peculiar velocity v_2D >= 25 km/s (Section 4.3 and Conclusions).
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents these objects as runaway stars ejected from natal clusters, not as possibly unbound from the Galaxy. Their maximum 2D velocities are ~40 km/s, well below Galactic escape velocity. The paper does not assess bound/unbound status relative to the Galactic potential.

source_refs.context: Section 4.3 'Are bowshock stars runaways?' discussing runaway classification based on v_2D >= 25 km/s with BSS/DES regions all below ~40 km/s.
Conclusions stating 22% of bowshock stars with kinematic data are runaways based on v_2D > 25 km/s.
